# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: apps/v1 # for versions before 1.8.0 use apps/v1beta2
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: wordpress
spec:
  replicas: 3  
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: wordpress    
      tier: frontend
  template:
     metadata:
       labels:
         app: wordpress
         tier: frontend
     spec:
        containers:
           - image: wordpress:4.7.1
             name: wordpress
             ports:
              # use the port number defined in this container to expose this service 
              - 56022121
              - 80      
            volumeMounts:
              - mountPath: /var/www/html
                name: wordpress-persistentvolumeclaim
        volumes:
          - name: wordpress-persistentvolumeclaim
            # use a dynamic PersistentVolumeClaim for the data
            persistentVolumeClaim:
              claimName: wordpress-pvc
---
apiVersion: v1 # for versions before 1.8.0 use v1beta2
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: wordpress-service  
  labels:
    app: wordpress        
      tier: frontend
  ports: 
  - port: 56022121      
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 80     # this matches the port number defined in the application container
spec:
  type: ClusterIP        # use a ClusterIP type for a service that will not be exposed externally.
  ports:                  
  - name: wordpress-service     
      port: 56021231         
    protocol: TCP        
  selector:          
     app: wordpress     
       tier: frontend
---
apiVersion: v1     # for versions before 1.8.0 use v1beta1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim   # this is not the type of PV
metadata:                 
  name: wordpress-pvc
spec:
  accessModes:         
    - ReadWriteOnce   
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi
